questions.description: "Following the enactment of the Companies Act 2014, the doctrine of Ultra Vires has been abolished for Ltds, to which…
questions.text: "Following the enactment of the Companies Act 2014, the doctrine of Ultra Vires has been abolished for Ltds, to which Parts 2 to 15 applies, but remains in place for DACs, PLCs and CLGs." You are required to discuss and explain this statement. In you answer you must cite all relevant case and statue law.

questions.description: "When proceedings are brought against the director of an insolvent company under Section 819 of the Companies Act 2014,…
questions.text: "When proceedings are brought against the director of an insolvent company under Section 819 of the Companies Act 2014, case law indicates that unless the director can show that he or she acted honestly and responsibly in relation to the conduct of the affairs of the company, the Court will make a restriction order." You are required to discuss this statement citing all relevant case and stature law in your answer.

questions.description: Paul is a director of Cars4All Limited (the "Company") which operates a… Company, Shareholder.
questions.text: Paul is a director of Cars4All Limited (the "Company") which operates a car sales business in South County Dublin. Paul is not a shareholder in the Company which is owned by a family consortium headed-up by Simon, although Paul has worked in the Company for 10 years. He worked first as a car salesman before being promoted on a number of occasions until finally being appointed a director of the Company with responsibility for sales. There are three other directors (all family members, making four directors in total). Simon, the majority shareholder and Managing Director of the Company, approaches you for advice. It has come to his knowledge that Paul has been "double-jobbing" for the last two years, selling second-hand cars through a website fronted by his wife, Jackie. Specifically, Simon has heard that Jackie has been buying cars from Paul that were traded in to the Company, at a discounted price before selling them on for a significant profit on their website; and Paul has been using the Company's garage-workshop to service the cars late at night and on Sundays when the garage-workshop was closed. He has used spare parts belonging to the Company but has not reimbursed the Company for these. You are required to advise Simon on the following: a) Whether you believe there have been any breaches of the duties owed to the Company by its director, Paul, under Part 5 of the Companies Act 2014; and b) The remedies that are available to the Company arising from any breaches of such duties. In your answer you must cite all relevant case and statute law and attempt to apply these to the facts provided above.

questions.description: Damien is the sole shareholder and one of the two directors of DRK Limited… Company, Shareholder.
questions.text: Damien is the sole shareholder and one of the two directors of DRK Limited (the "Company"), the other director of which is his wife, Deirdre. The Company operated the business of travel agent for a number of years. The Company has loan facilities from Big Bank plc which are secured by a floating charge over the Company's assets, a fixed charge over its leasehold interest in the premises it rents, and the personal guarantees of its two directors, Damien and Deirdre. A number of customers were owed money by the Company arising from a mix up in holidays and one, Colin, was owed €5,000 and was constantly calling to the family home of Damien and Deirdre seeking payment. In the last few weeks, the Revenue Commissioners petitioned to have the Company wound up on the grounds that it was unable to pay its debts and the High Court granted that petition, appointing Jack Harmer as the liquidator. You are retained by Jack to advise as to the law relating to the following payments by the Company: a) Some three months before the petition was presented, Colin called at the directors' home seeking payment. Damien was out and Deirdre, who was concerned at Colin's ever-threatening manner wrote him a cheque for €5,000 in respect of a refund owed to him. (8 marks) b) The day before the petition was heard by the High Court, Damien transferred €30,000 from one account the Company had into its overdrawn current account, which both Damien and Deirdre had guaranteed. (8 marks) c) The day after the petition was presented, the Company's bank debited the Company's current account for €10,000 in respect of accrued interest on the Company's loans. (4 marks) You are required to advise Jack Harmer the liquidator on the law in relation to each of the three payments distinguishing a), b) and c). In your answer you are required to cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: "The causes of crystallisation of a floating charge are now well settled in Irish case law".
questions.text: "The causes of crystallisation of a floating charge are now well settled in Irish case law". You are required to discuss this statement and in your answer, you are required to cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: A statutory predecessor to Section 31 of the Companies Act 2014 has been described as giving rise to a statutory…
questions.text: A statutory predecessor to Section 31 of the Companies Act 2014 has been described as giving rise to a statutory contract of the most sacred character". You are required to discuss this statement, explaining the nature and effect of Section 31 of the Companies Act 2014. In your answer you must cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: Bob's Burger Bars Limited (the Company) operates a mobile burger business… Company, Shareholder.
questions.text: Bob's Burger Bars Limited (the Company) operates a mobile burger business which has six vans on the road, selling burgers and other fast food at events in the greater Dublin region. It owns a premises on the Long Mile Road in Dublin where it pre-prepares some food and stores ingredients etc. The Company employs 15 people and has been in business for some 10 years. The Company's sole shareholder and sole director, Bob, was contacted by the Company's bank this morning and was informed that the bank was freezing the Company's accounts because it had learned that the Company had been struck off the register almost a year ago and had been dissolved. Bob contacts you in a panic, and admits that he may have been remiss in arranging for the filing of the Company's annual return and financial statements. You are required to advise Bob on: a) How the Company could have been struck off the register and how it can be restored to the register; and b) What are the legal consequences of the Company being struck off the register and of the Company being restored to the register. In your answer, you must cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: Harry is an insolvency practitioner and was recently appointed by Big Bank… Company, Shareholder.
questions.text: Harry is an insolvency practitioner and was recently appointed by Big Bank plc (the "Bank") to be receiver to Totally PC Coffee Limited (the "Company") on foot of a debenture the Company had given to the Bank to secure its borrowings. The Bank is owed over €2,000,000 by the Company and the Company's principal asset is a unit in an industrial estate in North County Dublin where it mixes, packages and distributes coffee beans sourced from a fair-trade plantation in Brazil. The unit was acquired by the Company for €2,500,000 in 2007. Harry believes that the premises is currently worth in the region of €1,500,000 and has a firm offer for that amount. There is another interested party who has said to Harry that he might be prepared to offer €1,900,000 in another three months when he can raise the cash. Harry is also receiving telephone calls on a daily basis from Richard, the managing director and sole shareholder in the Company, asking Harry to make sure he gets the best price for the property. Harry is aware that Richard gave a personal guarantee to the Bank and is aware that if there is a shortfall between the price received for the property and the amount owing to the Bank, he as guarantor will be liable in that amount. You are required to advise Harry on his duties as a receiver, having particular regard to the duties owed to the Company, and also whether he owes any separate duties to a guarantor. In you answer you are required to cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: "While company directors have a fiduciary duty to act in the interest of their company, they also have duties to have…
questions.text: "While company directors have a fiduciary duty to act in the interest of their company, they also have duties to have regard to the interests of other persons; however, all such duties are owed to the company." You are required to discuss and explain this statement. In your answer you must cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: Bob, Patrick and Patty are directors of B-P-P Limited (the "Company")… Company, Shareholder.
questions.text: Bob, Patrick and Patty are directors of B-P-P Limited (the "Company") which runs in an estate agency. They are also shareholders in the Company and each holds 25 of the 100 issued shares, the other 25 shares are being held by John. John is a solicitor and has never been a director of the company, being content to just to be just a shareholder. The premises from which the Company operates is owned by John and leased to the Company at a very favorable rent for the Company. Bob, Patrick and Patty have traditionally recommended that purchasers and vendors of properties that go through the agency years John solicitor's practice for their conveyancing and this has been a very lucrative source of revenue for John. John would in turn recommend to his clients that they use the Company as their estate agent. In recent times, however, John's recommendations have been few and far between and Bob, Patrick and Patty have been recommending other firms of solicitors to clients of the Company as a seems to them that these other solicitors are in a better position than John to make cross-referrals back to the Company. On average, the Company would have made about 30 referrals to John every month; however, for the last six months, this has fallen steadily and last month, there were only five referrals to him. John was unsure as to why referrals were falling off and had blamed it on the economic down turn until one of his friends in another firm of solicitors (who did not know John was a shareholder in the Company) mentioned that he and the Company were cross-referring clients very successfully. John was very annoyed and confronted Bob, Patrick and Patty claiming that in ceasing to maintain his levels of referrals they were acting in disregard of his interests as a shareholder, and he pointed to the below market rent he charged the Company for its premises. John also objected to not being told about their decision to make referrals elsewhere. Their response was that they were acting in the interests of the Company and they had never asked for a lower rent, and that he was welcome to seek to review the rent if he thought it was not at market value. They also indicated that this that if this was John's attitude, they would not make any referrals, to him a statement which provoked John to claim that they were acting oppressively. You are required to advise John as to the following matters: (a) whether John has a remedy under section 212 of the Companies Act 2014 (15 marks) [And]{.underline} (b) whether your answer will be different if the constitution or a shareholders' agreement contained the provision that the Company would support shareholders' businesses by referring clients to them. (5 marks) (c) You are required to answer [both]{.underline} part (a) (which carries 15 marks) and part (b) (which carries 5 marks). In your answer you must cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: "It is now well recognized that company's members can give their sanction to a course of action in either of three…
questions.text: "It is now well recognized that company's members can give their sanction to a course of action in either of three ways: first, by resolution passed at a general meeting of the company, duly convened and held; Secondly by unanimous or majority written resolution and thirdly, by an estoppel, created through their unanimous informal agreement." You are required to discuss this statement citing all relevant case and statue law in your answer.

questions.description: William's World of Colors Limited (the "Company") is a company engaged in… Company, Director.
questions.text: William's World of Colors Limited (the "Company") is a company engaged in the business of manufacturing and distributing paint to the Irish construction industry. For the past number of years, business has been booming. Two of the Company's customers have had lucrative contracts for the painting of new housing estates. The Company has three directors -William, Peggy and Eugene- and is a single-member private limited company, all shares being owned by William. William is the son of the Company's founder Ann is a managing director. Peggy, his daughter, is the Sales Director and Eugene is her husband. Eugene is a non-executive director who works as a local self-employed solicitor. His involvement with the Company is very limited and he never attends meetings of the directors. In recent times the Company's fortunes have faltered. Both of its major customers have gone into liquidation, owing the Company several hundred thousand euro. Both customers were always slow payers, but the Company received nothing from them for months although it continued to supply them with paint. William realised things were bad when the Company's bank manager telephoned him to say that the Company had exceeded its overdraft. William persuaded the bank to extend the overdraft to pay wages and give him time to collect some outstanding debts. The Company chugged along for another month but then William realised the Company was insolvent and without any hope of immediate improvement. At that point William place the company in the creditor's voluntary liquidation. The Company's liquidator discovered that the Company's record keeping was sporadic and that management accounts had not been prepared with the frequency that would have alerted the Company's directors to the fact it's two main customers had not been discharging what was owed by them for some time. Moreover, it seems that the Company had not been paying the Revenue Commissioners the PAYE and PRSI it had collected from its employees and owed the Revenue some €300,000. You are required to advise on the likelihood that each of the three directors will be made the subject of a restriction order pursuant to section 819 of the companies Act 2014 and on the defenses available to the directors. In your answer you must cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: "In addition to actual or deemed authority, the authority to bind the company can arise through a director having…
questions.text: "In addition to actual or deemed authority, the authority to bind the company can arise through a director having ostensible authority and third parties seeking to enforce contracts against companies entered into by directors may also rely upon the Rule in Turquand's Case". you are required to discuss this statement in the context of section 40 of the Companies Act 2014 setting all relevant case and statute law in your answer.

questions.description: "Section 602 of the Companies Act 2014 is a statutory example of the pari passu principle which requires all creditors…
questions.text: "Section 602 of the Companies Act 2014 is a statutory example of the pari passu principle which requires all creditors of an insolvent company to be placed on an equal footing and not "leapfrog" to statutory queue ordained by the Companies Act 2014". You are required to discuss this statement citing all relevant case and statute law in your answer.

questions.description: Pat is a plaster from Palmerston.
questions.text: Pat is a plaster from Palmerston. He operates as a sole trader and employees two staff. They work with Pat plastering new build houses and extensions. Recently, Peter, one of Pat's employees, narrowly avoided serious injury when the stilts he was wearing snagged on a hole in the floor. Only for Pat being in close proximity and able to catch him, the employee could have been seriously injured as he would have fallen into an open basement on the property and plunged four metres to the ground. This near miss has caused Pat to consider whether being a sole trader is such a good idea. Additionally, Paul, Pat's other employee has been pressing Pat to give him an interest in the business. Pat approaches you for advice, seeking information on (a) the advantages and disadvantages of incorporating Pat's business [and]{.underline} (b) the consequences of incorporation generally. You are required to answer both (a) and (b) in your answer you required to cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: David, Deborah and Cindy are equal shareholders, holding 50 shares each an… Company, Shareholder.
questions.text: David, Deborah and Cindy are equal shareholders, holding 50 shares each an Foxtrot Tango Limited (the "Company"), established by the late mother Madge. They received their shares under her will. The Company is in the business of retailing carpet and it's only two directors a David and Deborah who also work in the Company. Cindy left the country a number of years ago and lives with her husband in San Francisco and over the years has not had any involvement in the Company. Once a year she contacts her brother and sister to ask how the company is doing and usually, following that call, David and Deborah declare a dividend which the Company pays to Cindy, averaging over €50,000 per annum for the last three years. Last Christmas when Cindy came home to Ireland she tentatively required from David and Deborah whether they will be interested in buying her shares in the Company. They said she they would have to think about it because neither had cash on hand to buy her shares. David and Deborah come to you for advice when Cindy contacted him again and announced that she had sold her shares in the Company to Joe, a local businessman in the same town as where their Company has its premises. A couple of days later David and Deborah were approached by Joe who gave him a stock transfer form signed by Cindy and ask them to register him as a member. David and Deborah are unhappy to share the family business with an outsider and ask you for your advice. On examination of the Company's constitution, you see section 95(1)(a) of the Companies Act 2014 applies, and that the directors of the company may in their absolute discretion and without assigning any reason for doing so declared to register the transfer of their shares. You are required to advise David and Deborah on their rights and obligations in relation to the transfer of shares to Joe. In your answer you required to cite all relevant case and statute law.
